sanskrit:
विषयेन्द्रियसंयोगाद्यत्तदग्रेऽमृतोपमम्।
परिणामे विषमिव तत्सुखं राजसं स्मृतम्॥
transliteration: viṣayendriya-saṃyogād yat tad agre 'mṛtopamam | pariṇāme viṣam iva tat sukhaṃ rājasaṃ smṛtam ||
literal_meaning: From the contact of sense-objects with the senses, that which is like nectar in the beginning but like poison in the end is declared to be rājasic happiness.
contextual_meaning: Pleasure born of sense-contact is dopaminic: an anticipatory high that collapses into craving, regret, or numbness once the stimulus fades.
trigger_scenario: Scrolling late-night reels: each clip delights, then you notice 3 a.m. and an inner disgust.
anchor_text: What thrills at first and hurts later is rajasic joy.
